seo.title: 100 API-First Products resources for developers
seo.description: A curated set of practical API-First Products resources for engineers.

content.intro: Building an API-first product requires a shift from UI-centric development to focusing on developer experience (DX), predictable versioning, and scalable usage-based infrastructure. This resource guide provides the specific tools and patterns needed to design, secure, and monetize public-facing APIs.

sections.heading: API Design, Documentation, and SDK Generation

items.title: OpenAPI 3.1 Specification
items.description: The standard for defining RESTful interfaces. Use 3.1 specifically for better JSON Schema compatibility and webhooks support.

items.title: Scalar
items.description: An open-source alternative to Swagger UI that generates interactive documentation from OpenAPI specs with built-in API clients.

items.title: Spectral (by Stoplight)
items.description: A JSON/YAML linter to enforce API design rules (e.g., camelCase for properties, mandatory 400 error responses) during CI.

items.title: Fern
items.description: A toolchain that takes an OpenAPI spec and generates idiomatic SDKs for TypeScript, Python, Java, and Go, plus a documentation site.

items.title: tRPC
items.description: Ideal for end-to-end typesafe APIs where both the client and server are TypeScript-based, eliminating the need for manual schema definitions.

items.title: Zod-to-OpenAPI
items.description: A TypeScript library that allows you to generate OpenAPI documentation directly from your Zod schemas to ensure code-doc parity.

items.title: Mintlify
items.description: A documentation platform that parses code and OpenAPI specs to create high-performance, developer-friendly documentation portals.

items.title: Prism Mock Server
items.description: Use Prism to turn your OpenAPI file into a mock server locally so frontend teams can develop against an API before it's built.

items.title: Hono
items.description: An ultra-fast web framework for Edge environments (Cloudflare Workers, Bun) that provides built-in support for TypeScript and JSX.

items.title: FastAPI Pydantic V2
items.description: Leverage Pydantic V2 in FastAPI for high-performance data validation and automatic generation of interactive Swagger/ReDoc pages.

items.title: Unkey
items.description: Open-source API key management infrastructure that handles generation, verification, and rate-limiting at the edge.

items.title: Zuplo
items.description: An API Gateway designed for developers that handles authentication, rate limiting, and documentation with git-based workflows.

items.title: Cloudflare Workers Rate Limiting
items.description: Implement global rate limits at the edge using the `rate-limit` binding to protect your origin from abuse and DDoS.

items.title: Svix
items.description: A webhooks-as-a-service provider that handles the infrastructure for sending, retrying, and monitoring outbound webhooks to your users.

items.title: Upstash Redis for Global Rate Limiting
items.description: Use Upstash's serverless Redis and `@upstash/ratelimit` library for low-latency sliding window rate limiting across serverless functions.

items.title: Clerk API Authentication
items.description: Use Clerk to manage machine-to-machine (M2M) authentication and provide your users with a dashboard to manage their own API keys.

items.title: JWKS Endpoint Implementation
items.description: Expose a JSON Web Key Set (JWKS) endpoint to allow your customers to verify the authenticity of your signed JWTs or webhooks.

items.title: Standard Webhooks Spec
items.description: Follow the Standard Webhooks specification for payload signing and headers to ensure compatibility with modern consumer libraries.

items.title: Polar Proxy
items.description: An open-source tool for intercepting and inspecting TLS traffic, useful for debugging complex API-to-API communication.

items.title: Hoppscotch
items.description: An open-source alternative to Postman for testing and sharing API request collections within your engineering team.

items.title: Stripe Billing Metered Usage
items.description: Integrate Stripe's metered billing to charge customers based on the number of API calls processed in a billing cycle.

items.title: Lago
items.description: An open-source metering and billing engine that acts as an alternative to Stripe for complex usage-based pricing models.

items.title: Moesif API Analytics
items.description: A specialized analytics tool that tracks API usage per user/key, providing insights into which endpoints are most valuable.

items.title: Helicone
items.description: An observability platform specifically for AI API wrappers, tracking LLM costs, latency, and token usage for OpenAI/Anthropic proxies.

items.title: Orb Billing
items.description: A high-scale usage-based billing platform designed specifically for API-first companies with complex contract terms.

items.title: Propel Customer-Facing Analytics
items.description: Use Propel to build usage dashboards for your users so they can see their own API consumption and costs in real-time.

items.title: Inngest for Reliable Webhooks
items.description: Use Inngest to queue and retry external API calls or webhooks with built-in backoff logic and concurrency control.

items.title: OpenTelemetry (OTel) Tracing
items.description: Implement OTel instrumentation in your API to track requests across microservices and identify performance bottlenecks.

items.title: PostHog API Feature Flags
items.description: Use PostHog to manage feature rollout to specific API consumers based on their account ID or subscription tier.

items.title: ClickHouse for Usage Logs
items.description: Deploy ClickHouse to store and query billions of API request logs for internal auditing and billing calculations.

pro_tips: Always include an 'Idempotency-Key' header in POST/PUT requests to allow clients to safely retry failed network operations without side effects.
Implement 'Expand' parameters (similar to Stripe) to allow clients to request related resources in a single call, reducing total round trips.
Use a 'Deprecation' header in your API responses to programmatically inform developers of upcoming endpoint retirements before they break.
Adopt the 'X-RateLimit-Limit', 'X-RateLimit-Remaining', and 'X-RateLimit-Reset' header standards to help developers handle 429 errors gracefully.
Version your API through the URL (e.g., /v1/) for breaking changes, but use Stripe-style date-based versioning for granular backward compatibility.
